title: Stellar Wave 7 — ZK proof hook (optional differentiator)
goal: OPTIONAL / EXPLORATORY. Plan a zero-knowledge proof hook that attests Compass's co-signature corresponds to an evaluation that PASSED policy, without revealing the policy itself or the private inputs (amounts, thresholds). The proof sits between the brain's ALLOW decision (Waves 2-3) and the Wave 4 co-signature, bound to the exact co-signed transaction. PUBLIC = that a compliant evaluation occurred (and possibly the decision plus a tx commitment); PRIVATE = the policy rules, amounts, and thresholds. This wave is ONLY essential if the hackathon brief makes ZK a judged/central criterion; otherwise a ZK proof bolted on top would be DECORATIVE and must not be built. Solana keeps working unchanged and the brain stays untouched. Today there is ZERO ZK in the Compass codebase.

openQuestions.question: Which proving system should back the policy-compliance proof?
openQuestions.recommendation: OPEN. Do not lock in at spec time. Choose only if the decision gate opens, weighing trusted setup, proof size, prover latency, and tooling maturity against what a judge actually rewards.

openQuestions.question: What exactly is committed to bind the proof to the co-signed transaction?
openQuestions.recommendation: OPEN. Leaning toward a commitment over the canonical TransactionEnvelope XDR fingerprint used by Wave 4, so the proof and the co-signature cover the same tx. Concrete scheme TBD.

openQuestions.question: Where does the verifier run: off-chain or on Soroban?
openQuestions.recommendation: OPEN. Off-chain is cheaper and simpler but only moves trust; a Soroban verifier is a stronger story but adds on-chain cost and a new attack surface. Decide against the judged criterion, not by default.

openQuestions.question: Must the policy stay fully private, or is public-but-versioned acceptable?
openQuestions.recommendation: OPEN. If the policy can be public-but-versioned, much of the ZK motivation evaporates; full privacy is the stronger differentiator but harder. Resolve only if the gate opens.

openQuestions.question: What is the latency budget for proving, and must it run in-band with the co-signature?
openQuestions.recommendation: OPEN. The codebase reasons in terms of an COMPASS_LLM_TIMEOUT_MS-style interactive budget; a real prover may not fit in-band and may need an async/out-of-band path. Measure before committing to an in-band UX claim.

acceptanceCriteria: SPECULATIVE / GATED: a verifiable proof ties an ALLOW decision to a co-signature without exposing the policy or the private amounts/thresholds.
SPECULATIVE / GATED: the proof is bound to the exact transaction Compass co-signed (a commitment mismatch fails verification).
SPECULATIVE / GATED: a documented verifier exists (where it runs, what it checks, what it learns), even if off-chain only.
An explicit decision-gate sign-off is recorded in this task.json BEFORE any implementation begins.
Solana behavior is unchanged and the brain (policy engine, LLM judge, sanitizer, COMPASS_DECISIONS, MCP proxy) is untouched.
nonGoals: No production ZK system; any output is a demo/differentiator hook at best.
No claim that ZK is needed for the core co-signer thesis; Waves 0-6 stand alone.
No specific proving-system lock-in at spec time.
No change to any Solana provider or Solana signing behavior.
No on-chain (Soroban) verifier commitment before the decision gate and an explicit verifier decision.

decisionGate.rule: Do NOT implement this wave unless the hackathon brief makes ZK a judged/central criterion.
decisionGate.decorativeVsEssentialTest: If ZK is not judged centrally, a proof bolted on top of Compass is DECORATIVE and must not be built; the core guarantee already comes from Stellar-native multisig plus the policy-gated co-signer (Waves 0-6).
decisionGate.signOff: PENDING — no sign-off recorded. Implementation is blocked until this field records an explicit approval with the criterion that opened the gate.

tasks.title: Evaluate the decision gate before any implementation
tasks.description: Read the actual hackathon brief and decide whether ZK is a judged/central criterion. Apply the decorative-vs-essential test from exploration.md. Record an explicit sign-off (or a documented decline) in decisionGate.signOff. No further task may start until this is recorded.

tasks.title: Design the policy-compliance attestation (gated)
tasks.description: GATED on T1 sign-off. Resolve Q1, Q2, Q4: choose proving approach, the tx commitment scheme, and the policy privacy posture. Define the public/private split concretely so PRIVATE inputs (policy, amounts, thresholds) never appear in any public output.

tasks.title: Design and document the verifier (gated)
tasks.description: GATED on T1 sign-off. Resolve Q3, Q5: decide where the verifier runs (off-chain vs Soroban) and the proving latency posture (in-band vs async). Produce a documented verifier description: where it runs, what it checks, what it learns and does not learn.

tasks.title: Add RED tests for the attestation hook (gated)
tasks.description: GATED on T1 sign-off. Write tests before implementation: commitment binding (tx A proof fails for tx B), privacy (no witness element leaks into the proof/public inputs), gating (attestor never runs on DENY/ESCALATE), and isolation (removing the hook keeps Wave 4 co-signing tests green and Solana paths unchanged).

tasks.title: Implement the attestation + verifier hook (gated)
tasks.description: GATED on T1 sign-off and green-after-RED on T4. Implement the speculative contracts from technical-spec.md (zkAttestationContracts.ts, policyComplianceAttestor.ts, zkVerifier.ts) as an additive, fail-open-for-proof hook that never alters the decision and never blocks the core co-signer thesis.
